name: CLI / batch / automation utility
summary: A command-line or batch program driven by arguments, environment and stdin, emitting results to stdout/stderr with meaningful exit codes. Its distinctive concerns are scriptability, deterministic behaviour and a stable stream/flag contract rather than a graphical interface.

concerns: argument/flag design following POSIX/GNU conventions (short + long forms) with required --help and --version
discoverability: usage on error, worked examples, man page
clear, actionable error messages on stderr with remediation guidance
safe defaults for destructive operations (no destructive default; confirmation, --dry-run or explicit --force)
progress/feedback for long operations; TTY- and NO_COLOR-aware output
questions: Are flag/argument conventions, --help and --version specified per POSIX/GNU norms?
Are destructive operations guarded (confirmation, --dry-run, no destructive default)?
Are errors actionable, on stderr, with clear remediation?

concerns: deterministic, reproducible output for identical inputs
idempotent operations where applicable (safe re-run after interruption)
a documented exit-code taxonomy (0 success, distinct non-zero classes; 2 for usage errors by convention)
signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM) with safe interruption and no partial-state corruption
well-defined partial-failure semantics in batch mode
questions: Is the exit-code taxonomy defined (success vs distinct failure classes)?
Are operations deterministic and idempotent (safe to re-run after interruption)?
Is signal handling / safe interruption specified so no partial state is corrupted?

concerns: stdio contract: machine-readable data on stdout, logs and errors on stderr
pipeline composability (reads stdin, honours SIGPIPE, no interactive prompts polluting stdout)
a stable, parseable machine-readable output mode (--json/plain) that scripts can depend on across versions
config precedence (flag > env > config file > default) and XDG base directories
stability of the flag/output contract for downstream scripts
questions: Are stdout (data) vs stderr (logs) roles and pipeline composability (SIGPIPE) specified?
Is a stable machine-readable (JSON) output mode provided for scripts?
Is config/flag/env precedence (and XDG dirs) defined?

concerns: cross-platform and cross-shell behaviour (bash/zsh/PowerShell; path, line-ending and quoting differences)
packaging and distribution (single static binary, Homebrew/apt/winget/pip/npm) with checksums and signing
minimal runtime dependencies / self-contained build
locale-independent parsing and output for machine use (stable under LC_ALL=C)
questions: Are the supported OSes/shells and packaging/distribution channels specified?
Is the tool self-contained with minimal runtime dependencies?
Is machine output locale-independent (stable under LC_ALL=C)?

concerns: verbosity/log-level controls (-v/-q, --log-level) directed to stderr
testability via scriptable I/O and golden-output tests
structured/leveled logging kept separate from data output
reproducible, versioned release artifacts
questions: Are verbosity/log-level controls and structured logging (to stderr) specified?
Are CLI behaviours covered by golden-output / integration tests?
Are release builds reproducible and versioned?

concerns: no secrets on the command line (visible in ps/shell history) — prefer env, file or stdin
safe handling of untrusted input and filenames (path traversal, argument injection into spawned shells)
safe temp-file creation (mkstemp, no predictable paths)
supply-chain integrity of distributed binaries (signing/checksums)
least privilege: no unexpected network access or filesystem writes
questions: Are secrets kept out of argv (env/file/stdin instead)?
Is untrusted input (paths, shell arguments, temp files) handled safely?
Are distributed binaries signed and checksummed?
